content.text:
Hey, Can you check the feasibility for few things [no code change]- 
* I know we already have a backlog item to checkout DQX, can we make it bit more generic - use platform specific DQ engine if available (ex. - DMF for snowflake, DQX for databricks, DataPlex/CloudeDQ for BigQuery, etc.)? 
* Can we introduce tagging functionality in DataQ? if yes what will be the best approach to intoduce it?
* Since we intoduced assets, can we have a DQ scorecard for the assets [like how many types of checks configured - based on the dimentions. And how many checks are passing, with pre-defined or custom weights for each dimention and/or checks]?
You've hit your monthly spend limit · raise it at claude.ai/settings/usage
Continue from where you left off.

Picking this back up — the deliverable is a feasibility assessment (no code changes) for the three asks: a generic platform-native DQ engine seam, tagging, and an asset DQ scorecard. Let me ground it in the actual codebase and backlog first.

input.command: gh issue list --state all --search "DQX" --limit 10 --json number,title,state,milestone --jq '.[] | "\(.number) [\(.state)] \(.title) (\(.milestone.title // "no ms"))"'; echo ---; gh issue list --state all --search "tag" --limit 15 --json number,title,state --jq '.[] | "\(.number) [\(.state)] \(.title)"'; echo ---; gh issue list --state all --search "scorecard OR score" --limit 10 --json number,title,state --jq '.[] | "\(.number) [\(.state)] \(.title)"'
input.description: Search GitHub issues for DQX, tagging, scorecard items

input.prompt:
In /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ, gather facts (medium-thorough, read-only) to ground a feasibility assessment for three prospective features. Report concrete file paths, class/column names, and short excerpts.

1. ENGINE SEAM (for "platform-native DQ engine" idea — DQX on Databricks, Snowflake DMF, etc.):
- How CheckRunner is structured: backend/app/datasources/ — the CheckRunner interface/protocol, per-datasource runners (Snowflake, flat-file, UC, iceberg?), the shared gx_runner.py translation layer. What does a runner receive (suite, checks, connection?) and return (result schema)?
- The check model: backend/app/db/models — Check columns (kind, expectation type, kwargs, severity, thresholds?), Suite columns (does Suite have an `engine` field or anything engine-related?). Any mention of "dqx" or "engine" in backend or docs/adr.
- Result schema: results table columns (status, metric_value, observed_value, duration_ms etc.) — what an alternate engine would need to produce.

2. TAGGING:
- Any existing tag/label concept anywhere (backend models, migrations, frontend). Search for "tag" in backend/app and frontend/src (excluding html tags — look for data-model tags).
- What entities exist that would be taggable: suites, checks, connections, assets — list the model classes and their PK types.
- How list endpoints filter today (e.g., suites list, assets list — query params supported).

3. ASSET SCORECARD:
- The Asset model (from the #757 assets work): columns, how assets link to checks/suites/runs/results (asset_id on what?). The asset detail endpoint and what it returns. The lineage/asset services files.
- Whether checks have any "dimension" concept (completeness/validity/freshness etc.) — search for "dimension" in backend + frontend + docs. How would a check map to a DQ dimension today (expectation type? kind?)?
- The dashboard/aggregation service (dashboard_service?) — how pass rates are computed today (SQL aggregates over results?), whether per-asset aggregation exists.
- Check catalog: where the expectation catalog lives (catalog of check types) and whether it carries any category metadata.

Return an organized fact sheet; flag anything that contradicts my assumptions.

input.command: grep -rn -i "dqx" context/post-v1-roadmap.md docs/progress.md 2>/dev/null | head -20; echo ---; gh issue list --state open --search "Databricks in:title,body" --limit 10 --json number,title --jq '.[] | "\(.number) \(.title)"'
input.description: Find the DQX backlog item in roadmap docs and issues

content.text:
All three are feasible — none require reworking the core schema, and the codebase already has deliberate seams pointing at two of them. Here's the assessment, grounded in the current code (no changes made).

---

## 1. Generic "platform-native DQ engine" (DQX, Snowflake DMF, Dataplex…)

**Verdict: feasible, and the seam is already 80% shaped for it — but "native engine" hides two genuinely different integration patterns, and the design should say so explicitly.**

What exists today: `CheckRunner` is a protocol in [base.py](backend/app/datasources/base.py) (`run_checks(table, schema, checks) → SuiteOutcome`), dispatched per connection type via `_RUNNER_BUILDERS` in [registry.py](backend/app/datasources/registry.py). The UC runner's docstrings explicitly name DQX as the swap-in, and the post-v1 roadmap carries it as ADR 0003's v1.1 lane plus the G-g "engine-abstraction watch item" — there's no dedicated GitHub issue yet, which is worth fixing regardless. Notably, **nothing stores an engine choice**: engine is implicit in `connection.type`. Generalizing means an additive `Suite.engine` column (default `'gx'`) and two-key dispatch `(conn_type, engine) → runner builder`, plus a capability registry (which engines each conn type supports, and which check types each engine can translate). That part is small and backward-compatible.

The real fork is in engine *semantics*:

- **Synchronous evaluators (DQX, ad-hoc DMF calls)** fit the existing seam. DQX is rules-in → per-rule outcomes-out, which maps cleanly to `CheckSpec → CheckOutcome`. Caveat: the current UC runner pulls the table into pandas locally; DQX's whole value is Spark-side execution in the workspace, so a proper DQX integration is "submit rules to Databricks and collect results," not "swap the library" — a bigger lift than the docstring implies. Snowflake DMFs *can* also run this way (they're callable in a plain `SELECT`, returning a scalar), which maps beautifully onto the existing `metric_value` column with DataQ applying thresholds — and thresholds are already applied by the run service, not the runner, so that division of labor already works.
- **Warehouse-scheduled monitors (DMF schedules on tables, Dataplex/CloudDQ scans)** do *not* fit `run_checks`. There, the platform runs the checks on its own cadence and DataQ's job is to **provision attachments and ingest results** — structurally the same pull pattern you already built for orchestration polling and warehouse lineage (this branch!). That should be a second seam (think `NativeMonitorProvider`), landing rows into `results` with `metric_value` via the monitor-kind seam, not a `CheckRunner` impl.

One more honest constraint: Dataplex presupposes **BigQuery as a datasource**, which DataQ doesn't have — that's the dominant cost of that row, not the engine seam. My recommendation: write an ADR that (a) adds `Suite.engine` + the capability registry, (b) names the two patterns above, and (c) sequences DQX (sync, UC, already-promised) first, ad-hoc DMF second (cheap, high leverage since Snowflake is your flagship datasource), scheduled-DMF ingest third, Dataplex only with a BigQuery datasource decision. Per-engine, the recurring cost is the check-type translation matrix and normalizing outcomes into `RESULT_STATUSES` — that matrix is the thing to keep small and explicit.

## 2. Tagging

**Verdict: feasible, fully greenfield, and cheap — the main work is one design decision.**

There is no tag concept anywhere in the data model today (every "tag" hit in the frontend is the antd `<Tag>` component). All candidate entities — `Asset`, `Connection`, `Suite`, `Check` — have UUID PKs, so both classic shapes work:

- **Polymorphic**: one `tags` table + one `entity_tags(entity_type, entity_id, tag_id)` table. Fewer tables, but no real FK to the tagged entity — orphan rows on delete become your problem. Given the suite-delete FK cascade bug you already got burned by (#540), I'd avoid this.
- **Per-entity join tables** (recommended): `tags` + `suite_tags` / `asset_tags` / `connection_tags` / `check_tags`, each with real FKs and `ON DELETE CASCADE`. Four small tables, referential integrity for free, purely additive migration — clean under the backward-compat rule.

Recommended v1 scope: flat, workspace-shared labels (unique name, free-create by editors), `tags: [str]` added to read models, tag assignment via the existing PATCH endpoints, and a `tag=` filter param on the list endpoints. Note the list endpoints have essentially no filtering today (`/suites` filters only by `connection_id`, `/assets` not at all), so the filter is new-but-trivial WHERE clauses. Frontend is a natural fit — antd `Select mode="tags"` is already in use elsewhere.

Two design cautions: **don't conflate this with #433** (warehouse-native PII/PHI tags) — that's ingesting Snowflake/UC object tags as classification, a different trust model; but if you think you'll ever ingest those, shape `tags` as optionally key:value (like Snowflake object tags) rather than repainting later. And **don't implement DQ dimensions as tags** — dimensions want to be a structured field (see below), tags are the free-form axis. Effort: roughly one backend PR + one UI PR + one filters PR.

## 3. Asset DQ scorecard (dimension-based, weighted)

**Verdict: feasible and well-supported — about 60% of the substrate exists. The one hard prerequisite is exactly your open issue [#124](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/124) (DQ-dimension classification on checks).**

What already exists:
- A per-asset rollup: `AssetSummaryRead` already carries `checks_total`, `checks_passed`, `worst_severity`, and operational flags, computed by `asset_view_service._roll_up` over composing suites' latest runs. `Run.asset_id` is stamped at dispatch, so per-asset aggregation over `results` is a straight SQL join — no schema change needed for the "how many passing" half.
- A scoring formula: `dashboard_service` has the ADR-0005 severity-weighted health score (`pass=0, warn=0.5, fail=1, critical=2`) and `pass_rate` — but suite-scoped, and it's a **parallel implementation** to the asset rollup. Unifying those two paths is a prerequisite refactor worth doing anyway.

What's missing:
- **Dimensions (#124).** No dimension exists on `Check`; the closest thing is the frontend-only `ExpectationCategory` in `expectationCatalog.ts` (there is deliberately no backend catalog). Recommended shape: a `dimension` column on `Check` with a backend derivation function as the default — `kind=freshness` → Timeliness, `kind=volume` → Completeness/Volume, null/missing expectations → Completeness, `unique` → Uniqueness, range/regex/set → Validity, `comparison` → Consistency, `schema_drift` → Schema — plus a stored per-check override. Derivation-with-override means existing checks classify themselves on day one and the migration stays additive.
- **Weights.** Recommend v1 = per-dimension weights only (workspace default, optional per-asset override, stored as config/JSONB) — score = Σ(dimension pass-rate × weight)/Σweights, reusing the severity penalty within a dimension. Per-check weights are a lot of UI and explanation for marginal value; defer them.
- The genuinely valuable half is the **coverage view**: "this asset has zero Uniqueness or Timeliness checks" is more actionable than the score itself, and it falls out of the same dimension column for free.

One design decision to make consciously: asset visibility is derived from suite grants, and your #845 lesson was "don't ship a lie to fix a leak." A per-viewer score (computed only over suites the viewer can see) gives different users different numbers for the same asset; a workspace-true score computed over *all* composing suites is honest but must not break down suites the viewer can't see. I'd recommend the workspace-true score with hidden contributions shown as redacted counts — consistent with the anonymous-lineage-node precedent you already set.

**Sequencing across all three:** #124 dimensions → scorecard → weights, with tagging fully parallel (and tags later becoming the grouping axis for scorecard views, e.g. "score by domain:finance"). The native-engine work is independent, and its `metric_value`-producing DMF/Dataplex ingests would feed the same scorecard once both exist.

Happy to turn any of these into an ADR draft or file the issues (a dedicated DQX/native-engine issue is missing today; #124 would want a rescope note linking it to the scorecard) — say the word.
